title: Checkbox, Radio & Toggle
intro: The three choice controls all use the same trick: a visually hidden native `<input>` carries the state, and a sibling span draws the box, circle, or track. That keeps them keyboard-accessible and form-submittable for free while every visual state is pure CSS off `:checked`, `:focus`, and `:disabled`.

blocks.body:
Reach for `wf-checkbox` when each option is independent — any number can be on at once. Reach for `wf-radio` when the options are mutually exclusive and the user must land on exactly one; radios in a set share a `name` so the browser enforces the single-selection rule. Reach for `wf-toggle` when a single setting flips between two states (on/off, enabled/disabled) and the result takes effect immediately.

All three share one anatomy. A `<label>` wraps a hidden native input (`wf-checkbox-input`, `wf-radio-input`, or `wf-toggle-input`), a visual span (`wf-checkbox-box`, `wf-radio-circle`, or `wf-toggle-track`), and a text span (`*-label`). The input is hidden with `position: absolute; opacity: 0; width: 0; height: 0` — not `display: none` — so it stays focusable and keeps its native semantics. Wrapping the whole thing in a `<label>` means clicking the text toggles the control, and the box/circle/track is drawn by the sibling span reacting to the input's `:checked` state via the `+` combinator.

Stack checkboxes in a `wf-checkbox-group` and radios in a `wf-radio-group` — both are flex columns with a `--wf-space-sm` (8px) gap and an optional `wf-text wf-text-label` heading.

blocks.title: Checkbox states
blocks.caption: wf-checkbox across unchecked, checked, and disabled states. Checked fills the 18×18 box with --wf-color-primary (#2563eb) and reveals the SVG checkmark; disabled drops to the --wf-color-bg-disabled (#f1f5f9) fill and mutes the label.

blocks.title: Radio states
blocks.caption: A wf-radio group showing selected, unselected, and disabled options in one choice set. Selected fills the circle with --wf-color-primary and pops the 8px inner dot; the shared name attribute keeps only one selected at a time.

blocks.title: Toggle states
blocks.caption: The wf-toggle switch in off, on, and disabled states. The 44×24 track turns --wf-color-primary when on and slides the 20px knob 20px to the right via translateX.

blocks.title: Checkbox markup
blocks.intro: wf-checkbox state is driven entirely by attributes on the native input — add `checked`, add `disabled`, or pair them. There is no separate class per state.

rows.name: wf-checkbox
rows.when: The label wrapper. Click anywhere inside it — box or text — to toggle.

rows.name: checked (attribute)
rows.when: Pre-selected option. Adds the checked attribute; the box fills with --wf-color-primary and the SVG checkmark fades in.

rows.name: disabled (attribute)
rows.when: Non-interactive option. Add disabled to the input — the box drops to the disabled fill and the sibling label greys to --wf-color-text-disabled.

rows.name: wf-checkbox-group
rows.when: Two or more related checkboxes. Flex column, 8px gap, with an optional wf-text wf-text-label heading.
rows.code: Select Products

blocks.title: Radio markup
blocks.intro: Radios only behave as a single-selection set when every input in the group shares the same name attribute. Forget the name and each radio becomes independently checkable — the most common radio bug.

rows.name: wf-radio
rows.when: One option in a choice set. The circle is drawn by wf-radio-circle; the inner dot is its ::after.

rows.name: shared name (attribute)
rows.when: Always. Every radio in the same set carries the identical name so the browser enforces one selection.

rows.name: disabled (attribute)
rows.when: An option that exists but can't be picked — e.g. an unavailable shipping speed shown for completeness.

rows.name: wf-radio-group
rows.when: The required wrapper for a choice set. Flex column, 8px gap, optional wf-text wf-text-label heading.
rows.code: Contract Type

blocks.title: Toggle markup
blocks.intro: The toggle is a checkbox under the hood — the input is type="checkbox", and on/off maps to the checked attribute. The visual difference is entirely the track-and-knob span.

rows.name: wf-toggle
rows.when: A single immediate-effect setting. The 44×24 track and sliding knob are both drawn by wf-toggle-track.

rows.name: checked (attribute)
rows.when: On state. The track turns --wf-color-primary and the knob slides 20px right via translateX(20px).

rows.name: disabled (attribute)
rows.when: A setting that's locked — e.g. a feature flag gated behind an upgrade. Track drops to the disabled fill, knob recolors to the border grey.

blocks.title: Shared state tokens
blocks.intro: All three controls draw their selected, focus, and disabled treatments from the same handful of tokens, so a checked checkbox, a selected radio, and an on toggle all read as the same blue.

rows.use: The selected/on fill for all three — checkbox box, radio circle, toggle track.
Resting border on box and circle; the off-state toggle track fill.
Border on hover for box and circle; track fill on toggle hover.
Border color when the hidden input is focused.
The focus ring on all three controls, applied to the visual span via :focus + sibling.
Fill for disabled box, circle, and track.
Muted label color when the control is disabled.
Checkbox box corner radius — the only square control.
Radio circle, radio inner dot, toggle track, and toggle knob — everything round.

blocks.title: Sizing & accessibility floor
blocks.intro: From DESIGN.md §5.3 and §6.6 — floor requirements, not suggestions. A wireframe that fails these is rejected even if it looks polished.

items.rule: Interactive tap targets stay at 44×44 minimum, even at enterprise density.
items.why: DESIGN.md §5.3 / §6.6: compactness applies to spacing and text, never to hit boxes. The 18×18 box and circle and the 44×24 track are only the visual mark — the clickable target is the whole label, so keep the label height and padding at the 44×44 floor.

items.rule: Hide the native input with opacity/width/height, never display:none.
items.why: display:none removes the input from the tab order and the accessibility tree. The CSS uses position:absolute; opacity:0; width:0; height:0 precisely so the control stays keyboard-focusable and form-submittable.

items.rule: Every radio in a set shares one name attribute.
items.why: The name is what makes the browser enforce single-selection. Without it, each radio toggles independently and the choice set is broken.

items.rule: Keep the visible focus ring — don't strip it.
items.why: DESIGN.md §6.2: the :focus + span rule paints --wf-shadow-focus on the box, circle, or track. That ring is the only signal a keyboard user gets that the control is focused.

items.do: Wrap the input, visual span, and label inside one <label>.
items.dont: Put the <input> and a separate <span> as siblings outside a label.
items.why: The label association is what makes the text clickable and what the + combinator relies on to draw the checked state. Break the order and the box never fills.

items.do: Order the children input → visual span → label span.
items.dont: Place the label span before the box/circle/track.
items.why: Every state rule uses the adjacent-sibling (+) and general-sibling (~) selectors, which only reach forward. The visual span must follow the input, and the label must follow both.

items.do: Use wf-checkbox for independent options, wf-radio for one-of-many.
items.dont: Use a row of radios when more than one can be true.
items.why: Radios enforce exactly one selection. If two answers can be valid at once, the user gets stuck — that's a checkbox group.

items.do: Use wf-toggle for an immediate-effect on/off setting.
items.dont: Use a toggle for a choice that only applies after a Save.
items.why: A toggle signals the change is live. If the setting needs confirmation, a checkbox inside a form reads more honestly.

items.title: No indeterminate state in CSS
items.detail: The checkbox stylesheet only handles :checked and :disabled — there is no rule for the indeterminate (mixed) state. Setting input.indeterminate = true in JS will not render a dash; the box stays visually unchecked. If you need a tri-state parent checkbox, you'll have to add the styling yourself.

items.title: The toggle track is 44px wide but only 24px tall
items.detail: wf-toggle-track is 44px × 24px, so the visual switch alone does NOT meet the 44×44 hit-target floor on the vertical axis. The 44px comes from the full label row — keep enough label height/padding around the track or the tap target falls short of DESIGN.md §6.6.

items.title: Toggle on-distance is hard-coded to 20px
items.detail: The knob is 20px and slides via transform: translateX(20px) when checked. The 20px slide is tuned to the 44px track and 2px inset — if you resize the track, the knob will stop short or overrun the edge unless you retune the translate value.

items.title: Disabled checkbox/radio keeps the resting border, not a darker one
items.detail: When disabled, the box and circle take --wf-color-bg-disabled fill but revert to the plain --wf-color-border (not border-hover). A disabled-and-checked control therefore loses its primary-blue fill entirely — it reads as a greyed empty box, so don't rely on color alone to show it was selected.
